zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Cornwall於大約1280年出生在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓時，她的父親Walter de Cornwall27歲，母親Joan Fitz Alan18歲。她於大約1307年12月14日與James Peverell結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1348年8月1日在英國享年69歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭可瓦爾博德明。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cornwall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Corney
meanings: habitational name from Corney in Cumbria or Corney in Hertfordshire (now known as Corneybury), from Old English corn ‘grain’ or corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + ēg ‘island’. It seems possible, from the distribution of early forms, that it may also derive from a lost place in Lancashire. Alternatively, a habitational name from Corney (Lancashire), later named Corneyrow, now Corner Row or Cornoe in Greenhalgh (alias Greenall), in the parish of Kirkham. Corney was sometimes pronounced and spelled Cornah, Corner, Cornall, Gorney, and Gornall.
perhaps a shortened form of   Cornwall   or   Cornish  , or a metathesized form of   Croney  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Cornwall was born about 1280, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Walter de Cornwall, was 27 and her mother, Joan Fitz Alan, was 18. She married James Peverell about 14 December 1307. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 1 August 1348, in England, at the age of 69, and was buried in Bodmin, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Cornwall est née environ 1280, à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Walter de Cornwall, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Joan Fitz Alan, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé James Peverell environ 14 décembre 1307. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 1 août 1348, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Bodmin, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat um 14. Dezember 1307 James Peverell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 1. August 1348 im Alter von 69 Jahren in England und wurde in Bodmin, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Cornwallが約1280年にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアソウサンプトンで生まれた時，父Walter de Cornwallは27歳，母Joan Fitz Alanは18歳であった。約1307年12月14日にJames Peverellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1348年8月1日にイギリスで69歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスコルンワルボドミンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Cornwall(이)가 대략 1280년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter de Cornwall(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Fitz Alan(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 대략 1307년 12월 14일 James Peverell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1348년 8월 1일 영국에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 보드민에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com James Peverell aproximadamente 14 de dezembro de 1307. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1 de agosto de 1348, em Inglaterra, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Bodmin, Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Cornwall родилась около 1280 г. в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Walter de Cornwall было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Fitz Alan, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Peverell около 14 декабря 1307 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 1 августа 1348 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности бодминь, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания.
